data.title: Mony Group Cancels Newly Repurchased Shares in Ongoing Buyback
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Moneysupermarket.com ( (GB:MONY) ) has issued an update.

Mony Group PLC has executed a share buyback transaction on 1 May 2026, purchasing 113,492 ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange and multilateral trading facilities from Morgan Stanley & Co. International. The shares were bought at prices ranging between 173.6000 pence and 176.3500 pence, with a volume-weighted average price of 175.3800 pence per share.

The company intends to cancel all of the repurchased shares, effectively reducing its share capital and potentially enhancing earnings per share for remaining investors. The move continues a previously announced buyback programme and signals management’s confidence in the business and its capital allocation strategy, which may support the group’s market valuation over time.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:MONY) stock is a Buy with a £2.15 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Moneysupermarket.com stock, see the GB:MONY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MONY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MONY is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (profitability, low leverage, and robust free cash flow) and attractive valuation (low P/E and high dividend yield). These positives are partly offset by weak technicals, with the stock trading below key moving averages and showing bearish momentum.

To see Spark’s full report on MONY stock, click here.

More about Moneysupermarket.com

Mony Group PLC, formerly known as Moneysupermarket.com, operates in the UK financial services and price comparison industry. The group’s platforms enable consumers to compare and purchase products such as insurance, credit cards, loans, and other personal finance offerings, targeting value-conscious retail customers and online financial shoppers.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Worldwide Healthcare Trust PLC GBP ( (GB:WWH) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Worldwide Healthcare Trust PLC has executed a further market repurchase of its own shares under an existing shareholder authority, buying 600,000 ordinary shares at 336.95 pence each. The repurchased shares will be held in treasury, supporting capital management flexibility and potentially enhancing net asset value per share over time.

Following this transaction, the trust now holds 235,269,462 ordinary shares in treasury, with 601,665,200 shares in issue and 366,395,738 voting rights outstanding. The updated voting rights figure will be used by investors to assess disclosure thresholds under FCA transparency rules, providing clarity on ownership reporting requirements.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WWH is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by volatile profitability and inconsistent cash flow, despite a strong, low-debt balance sheet. Technicals are moderately constructive over the longer term but mixed in the near term, and valuation is less compelling due to negative earnings and a modest dividend. Corporate events are slightly supportive but not a major driver.

To see Spark’s full report on WWH stock, click here.

Worldwide Healthcare Trust PLC is a London-listed investment trust focused on the global healthcare sector. The company invests in a diversified portfolio of healthcare-related equities and assets, offering investors exposure to pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical technology companies worldwide.

Spire ( (SR) ) has provided an update.

On April 29, 2026, Spire Inc.’s board of directors approved an updated indemnification agreement for all directors and officers, expanding protections for individuals serving in leadership roles. The agreement commits the company to indemnify these indemnitees to the fullest extent allowed under Missouri law against certain liabilities and expenses tied to claims arising from their service.

The updated arrangement also provides for the advancement of legal fees and expenses, subject to conditions, and ensures continued coverage under Spire’s directors’ and officers’ insurance policies. These changes clarify procedures for seeking indemnification and advancement, potentially strengthening leadership protections and supporting governance stability at the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (SR) stock is a Buy with a $100.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Spire stock, see the SR Stock Forecast page.

Valuation is a positive (moderate P/E and solid dividend), while technicals are modestly weak near-term. Earnings call commentary was broadly constructive with reaffirmed multi-year guidance, tempered by acquisition execution and financing/cost headwinds.

To see Spark’s full report on SR stock, click here.

More about Spire

Spire Inc. operates in the utilities sector as a regulated energy company, with a corporate structure that includes a board of directors and executive officers covered by directors’ and officers’ (D&O) insurance. data.title: Cboe Global Markets Announces Major Restructuring and Cost Cuts
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Cboe Global Markets ( (CBOE) ).

On May 1, 2026, Cboe Global Markets, Inc., a major operator of cash and spot markets, derivatives exchanges, and market data services under its Cboe Data Vantage unit, reported record first-quarter 2026 financial results, including a 29% rise in net revenue to $728.9 million and a 54% jump in diluted EPS to $3.66. Strong growth in cash and spot markets, a 32% increase in derivatives revenues on record index options volumes, and a 19% gain in Data Vantage revenues underpinned an expansion in operating and EBITDA margins.

Alongside the earnings, Cboe advanced its strategic realignment launched after a 2025 portfolio review, raising 2026 organic net revenue and Data Vantage growth targets while cutting adjusted operating expense guidance to $838–$853 million. The company announced further restructuring measures expected to reduce its workforce by about 20% when combined with earlier asset sales and wind-downs, incurring $36–$46 million in pre-tax charges largely for severance from the second to fourth quarters of 2026, and targeting $40–$50 million in annualized pre-tax cost savings to fund investment in core franchises and emerging areas such as financial event markets, tokenization, expanded clearing, and global sales and education efforts.

The most recent analyst rating on (CBOE) stock is a Hold with a $330.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cboe Global Markets stock, see the CBOE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CBOE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CBOE is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (expanding margins, robust cash generation, and improving leverage). Technicals also support the rating with a clear uptrend, though momentum is getting stretched. Valuation is a mild headwind due to a moderate P/E and low dividend yield, while the earnings call adds support but is tempered by slower 2026 growth guidance and strategic/competitive uncertainties.

To see Spark’s full report on CBOE stock, click here.

More about Cboe Global Markets

Cboe Global Markets, Inc. is a Chicago-based exchange operator focused on cash and spot markets, derivatives, and market data services through its Cboe Data Vantage business. The company generates revenue from trading in index options and other products, clearing services in the U.S. and Europe, and data and access solutions for a global client base of investors and trading firms.

On May 1, 2026, Cboe reported record first-quarter 2026 results, with net revenue up 29% to $728.9 million and diluted EPS up 54% to $3.66, driven by strong performance in cash and spot markets, derivatives, and a 19% revenue gain in Data Vantage. The company raised its 2026 organic net revenue growth and Data Vantage growth targets, cut adjusted operating expense guidance, and detailed the next phase of a strategic realignment that began after a 2025 review, including actions expected to reduce its workforce by about 20%, incur $36–$46 million in restructuring charges through 2026, and deliver $40–$50 million in annualized cost savings while reallocating investment toward core and emerging growth areas.

Average Trading Volume: 896,615

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $31.43B

data.title: Perella Weinberg Names New COO Amid Mixed Q1 Results
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Perella Weinberg Partners ( (PWP) ) has shared an announcement.

Perella Weinberg Partners reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $148.9 million, down 30% from a record quarter a year earlier, resulting in a GAAP pre-tax loss of $11 million and an adjusted pre-tax loss of $3 million, while GAAP diluted EPS came in at $0.02 and adjusted EPS at $0.05. Despite lower deal activity and fewer fee-paying clients, the firm highlighted strong client dialogue, a two-year high in announced and pending backlog, ongoing investment in senior talent, the acquisition of U.K. advisory boutique Gleacher Shacklock, a solid cash position with no debt, and $63.8 million returned to shareholders through buybacks and dividends, underscoring a strategy to scale and strengthen its competitive position.

The company also expanded its leadership ranks, appointing Chief Financial Officer Alexandra Gottschalk as Chief Operating Officer effective April 27, 2026, reflecting confidence in her nearly two decades of finance and accounting experience and her role in the firm’s 2021 public listing. Operating expenses declined year over year, particularly non-compensation costs, even as higher fixed compensation and equity amortization weighed on margins, while capital management remained shareholder-friendly with continued repurchases and a declared quarterly dividend of $0.07 per share.

The most recent analyst rating on (PWP) stock is a Sell with a $19.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Perella Weinberg Partners stock, see the PWP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PWP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PWP is a Neutral.

The score is held back mainly by financial-profile risk—negative equity and historically volatile results—despite improved 2025 profitability and positive cash flow. Technicals are supportive with clear upward trend strength, but valuation is relatively rich (P/E 31.31) and the earnings outlook remains dependent on converting a strong pipeline into completed large transactions.

To see Spark’s full report on PWP stock, click here.

More about Perella Weinberg Partners

Perella Weinberg Partners is an independent advisory firm focused on investment banking and financial advisory services, including mergers and acquisitions, financing and capital solutions. The firm operates globally, with a growing presence in Europe, particularly the U.K., and seeks to scale and diversify by geography, industry and product mix.

Average Trading Volume: 902,871

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $2.1B

Find detailed analytics on PWP stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Douglas Dynamics ( (PLOW) ) has issued an update.

On April 30, 2026, Douglas Dynamics amended its Fourth Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation in Delaware to add exculpation provisions that limit personal liability for certain officers, following stockholder approval of the change at its annual meeting on April 29, 2026. At that 2026 annual meeting, shareholders elected four directors to terms expiring in 2028 and 2029, approved the company’s executive compensation on an advisory basis, ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor for 2026, and backed the officer exculpation amendment, signaling broad investor support for the board’s governance and risk-allocation framework.

These governance changes may strengthen Douglas Dynamics’ ability to attract and retain senior executives by narrowing certain legal exposures while keeping oversight with shareholders through regular director elections. The strong affirmative vote on pay practices and auditor ratification further reinforces continuity in the company’s leadership, compensation design, and financial reporting oversight, which are key concerns for institutional investors and other long-term stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (PLOW) stock is a Buy with a $55.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Douglas Dynamics stock, see the PLOW Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PLOW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PLOW is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (notably improved free cash flow and improving leverage) and a supportive earnings outlook with upbeat 2026 guidance. These positives are tempered by stretched technical conditions (overbought signals) and a valuation that is not especially cheap at ~21.8x earnings, while the dividend provides some support.

To see Spark’s full report on PLOW stock, click here.

More about Douglas Dynamics

Douglas Dynamics, Inc. operates in the manufacturing sector, specializing in equipment and attachments for work trucks and related vehicles used in commercial, municipal, and industrial applications. The company focuses on North American markets where seasonal and vocational demand for snow and ice control solutions and other truck-mounted equipment supports recurring replacement and aftermarket sales.

Average Trading Volume: 239,827

data.title: Esperion agrees to $1.1 billion ARCHIMED buyout
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Esperion ( (ESPR) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On May 1, 2026, Esperion announced a definitive agreement under which funds managed by healthcare-focused private equity firm ARCHIMED will acquire the company in an all-cash transaction valued at up to about $1.1 billion. The deal will see shareholders receive $3.16 per share in cash, a 58% premium to the April 30 close, plus one non-tradeable contingent value right per share that could yield up to $100 million in aggregate milestone payments tied to U.S. net sales of bempedoic acid and bumetanide products.

The merger, unanimously approved by Esperion’s board and backed by committed debt financing from Pharmakon-managed funds, is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, after which Esperion will be taken private and delisted from Nasdaq. The structure preserves upside for equity holders through sales-linked CVRs while giving ARCHIMED full control of Esperion’s cardiometabolic franchise, and the company has canceled its previously scheduled first-quarter 2026 earnings call as it pivots operational focus to completing the transaction.

The most recent analyst rating on (ESPR) stock is a Hold with a $3.16 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Esperion stock, see the ESPR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ESPR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ESPR is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial durability (negative equity, ongoing losses, and negative free cash flow) and bearish-to-neutral technicals. Offsetting these, the latest earnings call and corporate developments point to improving operating momentum, clearer 2026 planning, and portfolio expansion via the Corstasis/Enbumyst deal, though one-time 2025 revenue benefits and higher expense needs temper the upside.

To see Spark’s full report on ESPR stock, click here.

More about Esperion

Esperion Therapeutics, Inc. is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and delivering cardiometabolic and rare/orphan disease therapies. The Ann Arbor-based group markets two oral, once-daily non-statin LDL-C-lowering drugs, leverages ACLY biology, and operates a broad U.S. commercial infrastructure with approvals in more than 40 countries.

This positioning has made Esperion a partner-of-choice for innovators seeking U.S. market access via acquisitions, in-licenses, co-promotions and revenue sharing arrangements. The company is also building a diversified pipeline targeting conditions such as Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis and renal diseases, aiming to expand its role in cardiovascular and primary care markets.

Average Trading Volume: 5,626,672

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $514.8M

See more insights into ESPR stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Strategy ( (MSTR) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 30, 2026, Strategy Inc. announced it would maintain an annual dividend rate of 11.50% on its Variable Rate Series A Perpetual Stretch Preferred Stock, effective for monthly periods beginning on or after May 1, 2026. The board also declared a cash dividend of $0.958333333 per share for the month ending May 31, 2026, payable on May 31, 2026 to shareholders of record as of May 15, 2026.

As of May 1, 2026, the company expects that this dividend will be treated as a non-taxable return of capital for U.S. federal income tax purposes, subject to individual shareholder circumstances. Strategy Inc. reiterated that its website dashboard remains a primary disclosure channel for broad public access to information on its securities, bitcoin holdings and key metrics, reinforcing its approach to ongoing investor communication and regulatory compliance.

data.title: Hanmi Financial outlines growth strategy and capital strength
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hanmi Financial ( (HAFC) ) has shared an announcement.

On May 1, 2026, Hanmi Financial Corporation released a slide presentation to analysts and prospective investors outlining its operating and growth strategies and reviewing recent financial performance, including first-quarter 2026 metrics and detailed information on its loan portfolio and capital position. The materials, shared in connection with an upcoming financial institutions conference, are intended as an informational update rather than a statement of materiality, underscoring management’s emphasis on sustained loan growth, portfolio diversification and strong capital ratios as it competes in key metropolitan markets.

The presentation also highlights the bank’s experienced executive team, long operating history and record of expansion through acquisitions, geographic growth and product-line additions, suggesting that Hanmi aims to reinforce its standing as one of the largest Korean-American banks in the U.S. while signaling to investors and regulators its ongoing focus on credit quality, non-GAAP capital metrics and community-based relationship banking.

The most recent analyst rating on (HAFC) stock is a Buy with a $35.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hanmi Financial stock, see the HAFC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HAFC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HAFC is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial fundamentals (de-risked balance sheet and steady profitability) and strong valuation (low P/E with a solid dividend yield). Earnings-call commentary adds support via improving NIM, efficiency, and capital returns, while technicals are constructive but tempered by overbought momentum signals.

To see Spark’s full report on HAFC stock, click here.

More about Hanmi Financial

Hanmi Financial Corporation, parent of Hanmi Bank, is a U.S.-based regional bank that operates 32 full-service branches, five loan production offices and three loan centers across states including California, Texas, Illinois, Virginia, New Jersey, New York, Colorado, Washington and Georgia. Founded in 1982 in Los Angeles as the first Korean-American bank, it focuses on metropolitan areas with high Asian-American and multi-ethnic populations and offers a diversified portfolio spanning commercial real estate, equipment finance, residential real estate and multifamily lending, supported by capital levels that exceed regulatory requirements.

As of the first quarter of 2026, Hanmi reported approximately $7.8 billion in total assets, $6.8 billion in loans and $6.6 billion in deposits, with a 9% compound annual growth rate in average loans since 2013 and a tangible common equity to tangible assets ratio of 10.11% that underpins its investment profile for stakeholders.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $889.9M
